Package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject

Annotation Interface Bean

@Target({METHOD,FIELD}) @Retention(RUNTIME) @Inherited public @interface Bean
Bean injection annotation.

Used on methods and fields of BeanStore-managed objects to denote methods and fields that override and customize beans used by the framework.

Example

// Rest resource that uses a customized call logger. @Rest public class MyRest extends BasicRestServlet { // Option #1: As a field. @Bean CallLogger myCallLogger = CallLogger.create().logger("mylogger").build(); // Option #2: As a method. @Bean public CallLogger myCallLogger() { return CallLogger.create().logger("mylogger").build(); } }

The name()/value() attributes are used to differentiate between named beans.

Example

// Customized default request headers. @Bean("defaultRequestHeaders") HeaderList defaultRequestHeaders = HeaderList.create().set(ContentType.TEXT_PLAIN).build(); // Customized default response headers. @Bean("defaultResponseHeaders") HeaderList defaultResponseHeaders = HeaderList.create().set(ContentType.TEXT_PLAIN).build();

The methodScope() attribute is used to define beans in the scope of specific @RestOp-annotated methods.

Example

// Set a default header on a specific REST method. // Input parameter is the default header list builder with all annotations applied. @Bean(name="defaultRequestHeaders", methodScope="myRestMethod") public HeaderList.Builder myRequestHeaders(HeaderList.Builder builder) { return builder.set(ContentType.TEXT_PLAIN); } // Method that picks up default header defined above. @RestGet public Object myRestMethod(ContentType contentType) { ... }

This annotation can also be used to inject arbitrary beans into the bean store which allows them to be passed as resolved parameters on @RestOp-annotated methods.

Example

// Custom beans injected into the bean store. @Bean MyBean myBean1 = new MyBean(); @Bean("myBean2") MyBean myBean2 = new MyBean(); // Method that uses injected beans. @RestGet public Object doGet(MyBean myBean1, @Name("myBean2") MyBean myBean2) { ... }

This annotation can also be used on uninitialized fields. When fields are uninitialized, they will be set during initialization based on beans found in the bean store.

Example

// Fields that get set during initialization based on beans found in the bean store. @Bean CallLogger callLogger; @Bean BeanStore beanStore; // Note that the BeanStore itself can be accessed this way.

Notes:
  • Methods and fields can be static or non-static.
  • Any injectable beans (including spring beans) can be passed as arguments into methods.
  • Bean names are required when multiple beans of the same type exist in the bean store.
  • By default, the injected bean scope is class-level (applies to the entire class). The methodScope() annotation can be used to apply to method-level only (when applicable).
Precedence (since 10.0.0):

@Bean acts as a programmable default, analogous to Spring's @ConditionalOnMissingBean. When a REST context resolves a framework-managed bean (CallLogger, EncoderSet, SerializerSet, ParserSet, ThrownStore, Config, VarResolver, HttpPartSerializer, HttpPartParser, etc.), the lookup walks the following tiers in order, returning the first hit:

  1. Overriding-parent bean store — Spring beans (in juneau-rest-server-springboot deployments, via SpringBeanStore), or any bean reachable through the configured overriding-parent bean-store chain.
  2. @Bean method/field on the resource class — registered as a regular bean-store entry, beating the framework default.
  3. Memoizer-backed framework default — built into the context as a default supplier.

In other words: a Spring @Bean of type CallLogger wins over a @Bean CallLogger method on the same servlet, which in turn wins over the framework's built-in BasicCallLogger. Non-Spring deployments have an empty overriding-parent layer, so the chain naturally collapses to @Bean > default.

Prior to 10.0 the order was @Bean > Spring > default. See the 10.0.0 release notes for migration guidance if you need to keep that legacy behavior (typically by removing the Spring @Bean or marking the @Bean method's type with a Spring-native override such as @Primary).

See Also:
  • BeanStore
  • WritableBeanStore

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    String[]
    description
    Optional description for the exposed API.
    String[]
    methodScope
    The short names of the methods that this annotation applies to.
    String
    name
    The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes.
    int
    priority
    Optional ordering value used when exporting ordered bean collections.
    String
    value
    Same as name().

  • Element Details

    • description

      String[] description
      Optional description for the exposed API.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      9.2.0
      Default:
      {}

    • methodScope

      String[] methodScope
      The short names of the methods that this annotation applies to.

      Can use "*" to apply to all methods.

      Ignored for class-level scope.

      Returns:
      The short names of the methods that this annotation applies to, or empty if class-scope.
      Default:
      {}

    • name

      String name
      The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes.

      For example, there are two HeaderList beans: "defaultRequestHeaders" and "defaultResponseHeaders". This annotation would be used to differentiate between them.

      Returns:
      The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes, or blank if bean type is unique.
      Default:
      ""

    • value

      String value
      Same as name().
      Returns:
      The bean name to use to distinguish beans of the same type for different purposes, or blank if bean type is unique.
      Default:
      ""

    • priority

      int priority
      Optional ordering value used when exporting ordered bean collections.

      Lower values are higher precedence. If both Order and this value are present on the same contribution source, Order wins.

      Returns:
      The priority value.
      Default:
      1073741823
